title: 10 best herbal teas for motion sickness

remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, the active compounds found in kava tea, have been shown to help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by binding to GABA receptors in the brain, reducing anxiety and calming the stomach.

Sedative properties: The sedative effects of kava tea can also help to reduce feelings of unease and discomfort caused by motion sickness, allowing the body to better adapt to changing surroundings and motions.

Antihistamine-like effects: Kava tea has been found to have antihistamine-like properties, which may help to block the release of histamine, a chemical that can exacerbate nausea and vomiting in response to motion stimuli.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Piper methysticum, also known as kava, teas helps with motion sickness because of its unique properties that work to alleviate symptoms. The tea's active compounds, such as kavalactones, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness. Additionally, kava's ability to regulate the body's natural balance and promote relaxation also helps to calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and dizziness. This natural approach can provide relief for those prone to motion sickness, making it a popular remedy.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones (specifically, Kavain and Dihydrokavain): These compounds have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate the nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.

Flavonoids (specifically, Kaempferol and Quercetin): These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the body's response to motion sickness, leading to a decrease in nausea and discomfort.
Sterols (specifically, Stigmasterol): This compound has a stabilizing effect on the stomach, which can help alleviate nausea and prevent vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Piper methysticum leaves are used to make teas for motion sickness due to their high concentration of kavalactones, which have been shown to have anti-anxiety and anti-nausea effects.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Piper methysticum contain kavalactones, which are believed to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms by reducing anxiety and nausea.

Barks: Piper methysticum barks have also been traditionally used to make teas for motion sickness, possibly due to their content of kavalactones, although the effectiveness is less well-documented than with leaves and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried roots of piper methysticum containing 4-8 grams of the plant material for each serving.
Boil one liter of water for five minutes to create a hot brewing liquid.
Add the dried piper methysticum roots to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Serve the warm piper methysticum tea immediately and let it cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.

remedy_properties: Passionflower alkaloids: Help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and nausea associated with motion sickness, by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.
Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as a natural antihistamine, helping to reduce the body's histamine response to motion sickness, which can alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and vomiting.
Saponins: Soothe the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms in the gut, which can contribute to nausea and stomach upset associated with motion sickness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, teas helps with motion sickness because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids and alkaloids that have a sedative effect on the nervous system. The tea's calming properties can help to reduce the body's stress response to motion, alleviating symptoms of nausea and dizziness. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia, which can contribute to motion sickness. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Passiflora incarnata tea can provide relief from motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate motion sickness by reducing inflammation and stabilizing blood vessels, which can alleviate symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.

Harman alkaloids: Harman alkaloids, specifically harmine and harmaline, in Passiflora incarnata tea may help counteract motion sickness by influencing neurotransmitters and reducing anxiety, which can exacerbate nausea.

Isophytol: Isophytol is a terpene found in Passiflora incarnata, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, such as nausea and vomiting, by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which are believed to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are thought to have a sedative effect, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce motion sickness.
Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried flowers and 1 part of dried leaves of the plant in question.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the dried plant mixture in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Discard the solids and add honey or sugar to taste if desired.
Allow the tea to cool before consuming it within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Daucosterol: This compound has been shown to have anti-nausea properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing vomiting and stomach upset.
Scopoletin: As a natural antispasmodic, scopoletin may help relax the stomach muscles and reduce the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal discomfort and bloating that often accompany motion sickness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with motion sickness because it improves blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function and reducing symptoms associated with motion sickness. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba tea may help stabilize and strengthen blood vessels, thus reducing the severity of nausea and dizziness experienced during travel or motion. This natural remedy can provide relief from motion sickness, making it a popular choice for those who suffer from it.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms.

Terpenoids (specifically, Ginkgolides): These terpenes have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-convulsant effects, which may help alleviate the nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone may help stabilize mast cells and reduce the release of histamine, a neurotransmitter involved in the body's response to motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid content and ability to reduce nausea.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make teas for motion sickness as they contain ginkgolic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots are also used to make teas to alleviate motion sickness due to their flavonoid content and potential to reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Gather 60-120 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in a clean environment.
Measure and mix 10-15 grams of ginkgo biloba with 250 milliliters of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse properly.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove solids.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature before serving it in tea cups.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine-like properties: Valerian tea helps to reduce histamine levels in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea, dizziness, and vomiting.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: The calming compounds in valerian tea, such as valepotriates, can help to reduce anxiety and stress related to travel or motion, making it easier to cope with motion sickness symptoms.

Gastroprotective properties: Valerian tea may also help to soothe and protect the stomach lining from irritation caused by motion sickness, reducing the risk of nausea, vomiting, and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, teas helps with motion sickness because of its natural sedative properties and ability to regulate the nervous system. The tea's active compounds, such as valerenic acid and isovalerenic acid, have been shown to have a calming effect on the body, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, including dizziness and nausea. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, valerian tea can help individuals feel more comfortable and composed during travel.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, a short-chain fatty acid, helps with motion sickness by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness.
Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene acid, exhibits sedative and anti-anxiety properties, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce the symptoms associated with motion sickness.
Valeranone: Valeranone, a sesquiterpene, has a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help alleviate the nausea, dizziness, and disorientation associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root contains valerenic acid and isotonides, which have a sedative effect and help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Roots: Valerian root has been traditionally used to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea associated with motion sickness.
Roots: The roots are also a rich source of valerenal, which has been found to have antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Choose high-quality valeriana officinalis roots or dried leaves for optimal tea preparation.
Measure one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the valeriana officinalis in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release its properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose particles.
Allow the tea to cool for a few minutes before consuming it slowly and mindfully.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's cooling effect helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the digestive tract and calming the stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint tea, such as menthone and limonene, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe an upset stomach and relieve discomfort associated with motion sickness.

Gastrointestinal soothing compounds: Peppermint tea contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid that have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to reduce symptoms of motion sickness such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with motion sickness because of its natural ability to soothe the digestive system and calm the mind. The menthol content in peppermint acts as a natural anti-inflammatory, reducing nausea and discomfort associated with motion sickness. Additionally, the refreshing and invigorating aroma of peppermint can help alleviate feelings of dizziness and disorientation, making it a popular remedy for travelers and individuals prone to motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has a calming effect on the stomach and helps to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by numbing the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and spasms.

Volatile oils (especially Piperitenone and Menthone): These volatile oils have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, reducing the severity of nausea and vomiting, and helping to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Royleanone (a terpenoid): Royleanone has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the severity of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by protecting the stomach and intestines from damage caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas because of their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are used to make teas because they contain menthol and other volatile oils that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Buds: The buds are used to make teas because they contain a higher concentration of menthone and menthol than the leaves, which can provide faster relief from motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh m x piperita leaves and flowers when the plant is in full bloom.
Rinse the leaves and flowers with cool water and gently shake off excess moisture immediately.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the fresh m x piperita leaves and flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh strainer and discard the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool for 2 to 3 minutes before serving and consuming immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Digestive stimulants: Ginger's digestive stimulants, including gingerol and galanolactone, help to stimulate digestion, relieve stomach discomfort, and prevent nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.

Vomiting inhibitors: The sesquiterpenes present in ginger tea, such as bisabolene and farnesene, have been shown to inhibit the vomiting center of the brain, reducing the severity and duration of motion sickness symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with motion sickness because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to calm the digestive system. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work together to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Ginger teas have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, particularly during travel by boat or car. The soothing effects of ginger help to regulate digestion and alleviate queasiness, providing relief for those who experience motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly [6]-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the stomach.

Shogaols: Shogaols, especially [6]-shogaol, exhibit anti-nausea properties that help combat motion sickness by inhibiting the release of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that can contribute to nausea and vomiting.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to have anti-emetic effects that help alleviate motion sickness by blocking the action of serotonin receptors in the brain, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the primary source of the active compounds that help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The bark of Zingiber officinale is also used to make teas, although it is less commonly used than the rhyzomes, it still contains some active compounds that help with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale root and place it in a clean tea infuser.
Steep the zingiber officinale root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids after 5 minutes of steeping time.
Add honey to taste and stir well to dissolve the sweetness evenly into the tea.
Serve the zingiber officinale tea immediately and enjoy as a warm soothing beverage.

remedy_properties: Linalool: This terpene helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea, making it easier for the body to digest food while on the move, thereby alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.

Linalyl acetate: This compound has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and discomfort that often accompany motion sickness, allowing the body to relax and better adapt to changing environments.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as stomach cramps, bloating, and diarrhea, all of which are common complaints during motion sickness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with motion sickness because of its calming and relaxing properties. The active compounds present in the tea, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a natural sedative effect on the nervous system, which helps to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing anxiety, Lavandula angustifolia tea can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, providing relief for individuals who experience discomfort during travel or other forms of transportation.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness by minimizing the body's response to the conflicting sensory inputs.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene ester, it has sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Lavandulol: This terpene has been shown to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help reduce the anxiety and discomfort often associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their calming and soothing properties, which help alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Flowers: Employed for their fragrance and medicinal benefits, which include reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation to combat motion sickness.
Stems: Utilized for their structural integrity and ability to infuse the tea with the plant's medicinal properties, contributing to its effectiveness in alleviating motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1/4 cup dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in a clean and dry container for storage.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers per 8 oz of water for the perfect brew.
Bring 8 oz of fresh water to a boil in a medium saucepan within 5 minutes.
Steep the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve immediately after steeping is complete within 2 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi tea's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms like nausea and dizziness.

Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: The herb Bacopa monnieri, commonly used in brahmi tea, has been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety and unease that often accompany motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and preventing further irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri, also known as brahmi, teas helps with motion sickness because of its ability to enhance memory and cognitive function, particularly in regards to spatial awareness and coordination. The herb's active compounds, such as bacosides, are believed to stabilize and adapt the body's response to motion stimuli, reducing the conflict between the body's sensory inputs and its motor responses, ultimately alleviating the symptoms of motion sickness. This natural remedy is a popular choice for those seeking a holistic solution to motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Bacopasides: Bacopasides are a group of saponin glycosides present in Bacopa monnieri that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Bacoside A: Bacoside A is a specific saponin glycoside that has been found to have neuroprotective effects, which can help prevent the development of motion sickness by protecting the brain and nervous system from damage caused by prolonged exposure to motion.

Bacopaside II: Bacopaside II is another saponin glycoside present in Bacopa monnieri that has been shown to have anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness by reducing anxiety and inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in bacosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and alkaloids that may help to calm the nervous system and reduce nausea associated with motion sickness.
Stems: Used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to help reduce anxiety and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness due to their potential to balance the body's doshas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or local herbalist.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the ground herb with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a second cup to remove any remaining solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool and then consume the entire amount within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Anethole: A natural essential oil found in fennel, anethole has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea and vomiting.

Ferulic acid: A phytochemical present in fennel, ferulic acid has been found to have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to motion sickness.

Chlorogenic acid: Another phytochemical in fennel, chlorogenic acid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects similar to anethole, which can help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, teas helps with motion sickness because it contains anethole and fenchone, compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system. These volatile oils may help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing the contractions of the stomach and intestines, promoting a sense of balance and reducing queasiness. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its efficacy in soothing digestive discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a sesquiterpene lactone, helps alleviate motion sickness by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce gastrointestinal upset associated with motion sickness.
Anethole: Anethole, a phenylpropene, may help with motion sickness by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help calm the nervous system and reduce nausea.

Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid, may help alleviate motion sickness by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are commonly used to make teas for motion sickness because they contain a high concentration of the compound anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are also used to make teas for motion sickness because they have a similar composition to the seeds and contain anethole, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting.

Roots: The roots of Foeniculum vulgare are sometimes used to make teas for motion sickness because they contain a higher concentration of volatile oils compared to the leaves and seeds, which may provide additional relief from nausea and digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 teaspoons of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from the plant's stems.
Rinse the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers with cool filtered water for 10 seconds.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the rinsed foeniculum vulgare with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the foeniculum vulgare mixture to steep for 5 to 7 minutes then strain the liquid.
Store the herbal tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving chilled.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by calming the digestive system.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: The soothing effects of chamomile tea's flavonoids and terpenoids can help to calm nerves and reduce anxiety caused by motion sickness, making it easier for individuals to cope with symptoms.

Gastroprotective properties: Chamomile tea's antioxidants and flavonoids have been shown to protect the stomach lining from irritation and inflammation, which is often exacerbated during motion sickness, thereby reducing the risk of nausea and vomiting.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with motion sickness because of its soothing and calming properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile tea, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce nausea and alleviate digestive discomfort. Chamomile's sedative effects also work to calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate motion sickness. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from motion sickness, making it a popular choice for those who experience nausea and discomfort while traveling.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a natural anxiolytic agent, reducing anxiety and stress levels associated with motion sickness.
Bisabolol: As a sesquiterpene, bisabolol exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.
Alpha-bisabolol oxide A: This sesquiterpene has been shown to have a sedative effect, which may help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness, including dizziness and nausea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make teas for motion sickness due to their calming and soothing effects on the stomach and digestive system.
Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make teas for motion sickness due to their ability to help regulate the body's response to motion and reduce nausea.
Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make teas for motion sickness due to their potential to help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers or 2 tablespoons dried flowers for tea preparation.
Rinse the flowers in cool water and gently pat them dry with a clean towel to remove excess moisture.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers or 2 teaspoons of fresh flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove the flowers completely.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon teas helps with motion sickness because it contains a unique combination of active compounds that work together to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea. The cinnamaldehyde present in Ceylon cinnamon has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract caused by motion sickness. Additionally, the tea's warm and comforting nature helps to calm the body and promote relaxation, making it an effective remedy for alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and cinnamic acid, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms like nausea and stomach discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of Ceylon cinnamon tea helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms, such as oxidative stress and inflammation.

Carminative properties: The volatile oils present in Ceylon cinnamon, including cineole and linalool, have natural carminative effects, which can help to reduce flatulence and alleviate bloating, two common symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Ceylon cinnamon plant contain compounds that help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Ceylon cinnamon have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 grams of fresh cinnamomum verum bark and grind it into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep the ground cinnamon in 250 milliliters of boiling water for exactly 7 minutes and 30 seconds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining cinnamon solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well, dissolving the sweetness evenly throughout the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature of around 80 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with motion sickness because of its active compound, curcumin, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, curcumin can help to alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach discomfort commonly associated with motion sickness. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects may also help to reduce the severity of inflammation in the digestive system, further contributing to its beneficial effects on motion sickness sufferers.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes in the body.

Volatile Oils: Turmeric contains volatile oils such as cineole, pinene, and limonene, which have natural antispasmodic and anti-nausea properties that can help soothe stomach discomfort and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Antioxidants: Turmeric's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from free radical damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Roots: Contain volatile oils and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried turmeric root curcuma longa powder into a small saucepan.
Boil one cup of fresh filtered water in the saucepan and add the turmeric powder.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.
Steep the tea for an additional 2-3 minutes and serve warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pennyroyal teas helps with motion sickness because of its natural properties that help to calm the digestive system and reduce symptoms such as nausea, dizziness, and vomiting. The essential oils present in pennyroyal tea, including menthol and pulegone, have a soothing effect on the stomach and can help to ease discomfort associated with motion sickness. Additionally, pennyroyal's mild sedative properties can also help to promote relaxation and reduce feelings of anxiety, which are common triggers for motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Essential oils: Pennyroyal tea contains essential oils such as pulegone, which have been shown to have anti-nausea and anti-emetic effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in pennyroyal tea help relax the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps associated with motion sickness, allowing for easier digestion and reduced discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Pennyroyal tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their calming effects, pennyroyal leaves are used in teas to soothe digestive issues and alleviate nausea associated with motion sickness.
Roots: Pennyroyal roots have been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal disorders, making them a popular choice for crafting teas that help relieve symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves mentha pulegium for every 8 ounces water.
Bring the water to a boil in a large pot then reduce heat to low simmer.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it immediately as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram teas helps with motion sickness because its essential oils, such as thymol and camphor, have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds help to calm the stomach and relieve nausea, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. Additionally, wild marjoram's mild sedative effects can also help to alleviate dizziness and vomiting, making it a soothing and effective herbal remedy for those prone to motion sickness while traveling by car, boat, or plane.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Wild marjoram tea helps to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Carminative properties: The tea's carminative effects help to dispel gas and relieve bloating, which can exacerbate feelings of discomfort and queasiness caused by motion sickness.

Gastric soothing properties: Wild marjoram tea contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antacid properties, helping to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Flowers: Contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried oregano leaves in sufficient quantity for desired strength of tea.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried oregano leaves with boiling water to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard solids immediately.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired and stir well before drinking.
Consume the tea 30 minutes prior to travel or as needed during motion sickness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose teas helps with motion sickness because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The tea's volatile oils, including geraniol and citronellol, help to relax the stomach muscles, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate dizziness caused by motion. Additionally, the rose petals' gentle sedative properties can calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness, making it a soothing and effective natural remedy for those prone to seasickness or car travel discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Damask rose tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which can help alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties in damask rose tea can help relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms.
Stomach soother: Damask rose tea has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: Damask rose seeds have been traditionally used to calm digestive issues, including those caused by motion sickness, due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried rose petals per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the rose petals in boiling water for five to seven minutes or as desired.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming for optimal benefits.
Drink one to two cups of cooled tea up to three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with motion sickness because of its unique blend of soothing herbs that calm the stomach and reduce nausea. The anti-inflammatory properties in elderflower tea help to ease digestive discomfort, while the peppermint and ginger components work together to alleviate queasiness and dizziness. Additionally, the calming effects of chamomile and lemon balm help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, further alleviating motion sickness symptoms. As a natural remedy, elder teas provide a gentle and effective way to manage motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant found in elder flowers, quercetin helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Vasodilatory compounds: The vasodilatory compounds present in elder flower extract, such as hesperidin and diosmin, can help to improve blood circulation and reduce the sensation of dizziness and disorientation that often accompanies motion sickness.

Alkaloids: Elder tea contains various alkaloids, including berberine and beta-sitosterol, which have been shown to have anti-emetic properties, helping to calm nausea and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: Contain mucilages that soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and flowers in sufficient quantities for brewing tea.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra per cup of boiling water or two teaspoons of fresh material.
Steep the herb mixture in hot water for 5-10 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before drinking or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with motion sickness because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive system and calm the stomach. The herbs in thyme tea, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the production of chemicals that trigger these symptoms. Additionally, the warmth and aroma of the tea can help to relax the body and mind, further reducing feelings of queasiness and discomfort caused by motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has natural antibacterial and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve nausea and stomach cramps associated with motion sickness.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, another key component of thyme, exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as headaches and dizziness.

Cineole: Cineole, a terpene found in thyme, has natural sedative and anti-nausea effects, making it effective in reducing the severity of motion sickness symptoms, including vomiting and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain volatile oils and flavonoids that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Leaves: Thyme leaves possess antimicrobial properties that can help soothe stomach upset and reduce inflammation caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried thyme flowers and leaves from thymus vulgaris plants.
Combine the thyme herb with one cup of boiling water in a large glass infuser or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the thyme's natural oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool for at least 10 minutes before drinking, ideally at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with motion sickness because it contains a calming and soothing essence that can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach discomfort. The tea's natural antispasmodic properties can also help to relax the stomach muscles, reducing cramps and spasms caused by motion sickness. Additionally, lemon verbena's citrusy flavor can be very uplifting, helping to improve circulation and reduce fatigue often associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic properties: Lemon verbena tea has been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by calming the stomach and gut.

Antispasmodic properties: The tea's antispasmodic effects help relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as cramping and discomfort.
Anxiolytic properties: Lemon verbena tea contains flavonoids that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress related to motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating symptoms of motion sickness.

Buds: The buds of the lemon verbena plant contain a higher concentration of essential oils than other parts of the plant, which helps to soothe digestive issues and calm nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers and place them in a heat-resistant teapot.
Heat 250 milliliters of boiling water for exactly 5 minutes in a separate kettle.
Steep the verbena mixture in the hot water for 7-10 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool for exactly 2 minutes before serving, ensuring optimal potency and flavor.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to motion sickness that are treated with herbal teas are nausea, dizziness, and anxiety. Peppermint tea is a popular remedy for digestive issues and can help soothe an upset stomach. Ginger tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties and can ease nausea and vomiting. Lemon balm tea is calming and can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and restlessness, while chamomile tea promotes relaxation and reduces symptoms of insomnia. These herbal teas offer a natural and gentle approach to managing these common complaints.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for motion sickness are a natural remedy that helps alleviate nausea and discomfort associated with travel-related motion sickness. These teas typically contain ingredients like ginger, peppermint, and lemon balm, which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties. Examples include ginger tea, peppermint tea, and meadowsweet tea. Drinking these teas before and during travel can improve lives by reducing anxiety and discomfort, allowing individuals to enjoy travel and daily commutes without the burden of motion sickness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with motion sickness is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and lemon balm. Peppermint tea helps to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea, while ginger tea provides anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate discomfort. Lemon balm tea, with its soothing and calming effects, can help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with motion sickness. Together, these teas create a natural and effective remedy to alleviate motion sickness symptoms and promote a sense of well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Acta oto-laryngologica, researchers used a controlled, double-blind method to examine the effects of ginger root on motion sickness. The study involved test subjects who were given powdered ginger root, a placebo, or dimenhydrinate, and their eye movements were recorded using standard ENG equipment. The results showed that while dimenhydrinate reduced nystagmus response to motion stimuli, ginger root had no influence on the experimentally induced nystagmus, suggesting that ginger root may help alleviate motion sickness symptoms through its effects on the gastric system.
